entities.name: SOIL
entities.description: Medium that acts as a major sink and source of atmospheric CO2 and plays a role in carbon capture and storage

entities.name: ATMOSPHERIC CO2
entities.description: Carbon dioxide present in the atmosphere that is captured or released by soils

entities.name: CARBON CAPTURE AND STORAGE
entities.description: The process of capturing and storing atmospheric carbon to mitigate climate change

entities.name: ORGANIC CARBON
entities.description: Carbon stored in soils through plant photosynthesis and decomposition of plant materials and roots

entities.name: INORGANIC CARBON
entities.description: Carbon stored in soils through the formation of pedogenic calcium carbonates

entities.name: PHOTOSYNTHESIS
entities.description: Biological process by which plants capture carbon from the atmosphere

entities.name: PLANTS
entities.description: Organisms that capture carbon via photosynthesis

entities.name: PEDOGENIC CALCIUM CARBONATES
entities.description: Mineral forms of inorganic carbon created within the soil

entities.name: CARBON SEQUESTRATION
entities.description: The long-term removal and storage of carbon in soils

entities.name: MANAGEMENT TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Appropriate practices required for the sequestration of organic and inorganic carbon

entities.name: SOIL TYPE
entities.description: The spatial distribution and classification of soil types used to estimate carbon roles

entities.name: SOIL ORGANIC CARBON
entities.description: The organic fraction of carbon found within the soil

entities.name: SOIL INORGANIC CARBON
entities.description: The inorganic fraction of carbon found within the soil

entities.name: BULK DENSITY
entities.description: The mass of soil per unit volume used to estimate carbon capture and storage

entities.name: AGROCLIMATIC ZONES
entities.description: Land area delineations used as base maps for estimating carbon capture and storage in India

entities.name: BIOCLIMATIC SYSTEMS
entities.description: Land area delineations based on biological and climatic factors used as base maps in India

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The geographical region where agroclimatic zones, bioclimatic systems, and agro-ecosubregions are mapped

entities.name: AGRO-ECOSUBREGIONS
entities.description: Detailed land area delineations used as base maps for planning and carbon estimation in India

entities.name: THEMATIC MAPS
entities.description: Maps on carbon stock used to prioritize areas for carbon sequestration

entities.name: GLOBAL CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: The environmental condition causing elevated atmospheric CO2 levels
